success_surface: Cross-sheet row deletion with multi-criteria matching while preserving formatting
effective_behaviors: Used two-phase processing: pandas for data logic, openpyxl for structural operations
Sorted row deletion indices in descending order before deleting to prevent index shifting
Explored spreadsheet structure with sample code before implementing the full solution
Validated matching criteria with explicit logging before executing deletions
Preserved original workbook by saving to new output path rather than in-place modification
Cleared existing sheet content completely before rewriting to ensure clean state
Handled null/NaN values explicitly by converting to empty strings during cell writing
evidence: Used pd.read_excel() for reading, then openpyxl load_workbook() for writing back
Applied sorted(list(pl_rows_to_delete), reverse=True) before loop deletion
Initial exploration code listed columns and shapes before implementing matching logic
Printed Match Found messages and row counts before and after deletion
wb_new.save(output_path) created new file instead of overwriting input_path
Loop ws_pl.delete_rows(row_idx) range(max_row, 1, -1) cleared data before rewrite
cell_value = value if pd.notna(value) else '' handled null conversion

memory_items.title: Hybrid Library Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for data analysis and filtering, openpyxl for structural changes
memory_items.generalization: When tasks require both complex data logic and Excel format preservation, process data with pandas first, then write results through openpyxl to retain styles and structure.

memory_items.title: Safe Row Deletion Ordering
memory_items.lesson: Sort deletion indices descending to prevent offset errors during row removal
memory_items.generalization: When deleting multiple rows with index-based operations, sort target indices in reverse order so lower-index rows are not affected by higher-index deletions.

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Exploration
memory_items.lesson: Probe data structure before implementing full transformation logic
memory_items.generalization: Before coding the complete solution, run diagnostic queries to confirm sheet names, column layouts, and data types exist as expected.

memory_items.title: Validation Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Log match findings and row counts at each processing stage
memory_items.generalization: Always report what will be changed before changing it, including row counts, matched records, and expected outcomes for verification.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Hybrid Library Pattern
- Use pandas for data analysis and filtering; use openpyxl for structural changes.
- Process data with pandas first, then write results through openpyxl to retain styles and structure.

### Safe Row Deletion Ordering
- Sort deletion indices in descending order before removing rows.
- Lower-index rows remain unaffected when higher-index rows are deleted first.

### Pre-Modification Exploration
- Probe data structure before implementing full transformation logic.
- Run diagnostic queries to confirm sheet names, column layouts, and data types exist as expected.

### Validation Before Execution
- Log match findings and row counts at each processing stage.
- Report what will be changed before changing it, including expected outcomes for verification.
